name: h5py (HDF5 for Python)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD 3-Clause); standard HDF5 library bindings

performance.notes: Near-raw I/O speed; chunking and compression configurable; parallel I/O via MPI

agent_readiness.notes: Python interface to HDF5 binary file format. Hierarchical data: groups (like directories), datasets (like NumPy arrays), attributes. Compression (GZIP, LZF, SZIP). Chunking. Partial I/O (read slices without loading full dataset). MPI parallel I/O. Dict-like API. Used for ML model weights (Keras .h5 format), scientific datasets, large numerical data. Well-established, battle-tested. Zarr is preferred for cloud-native use; HDF5 better for local high-performance I/O.

gotchas: Not cloud-native (requires full file access — no S3 partial reads)
Zarr preferred for new cloud workflows
best_when: Large numerical datasets requiring partial I/O
summary: h5py provides Python access to HDF5 binary files — hierarchical groups and NumPy-compatible datasets with compression and chunking. Standard format for Keras model weights (.h5), scientific datasets, and HPC numerical data. Battle-tested and performant for local high-performance I/O. For cloud-native array storage, prefer Zarr.
